note: ANALYST emits claims. Prose is rendered from PASSED claims at the EDITOR stage. No prose exists yet. Claims proposing a quote do not carry offsets; the VERIFIER binds offsets by locating the quote in the snapshot. A quote the verifier cannot locate is a fabricated citation and the claim dies.

claims.text: The FTC alleged that Illuminate stored student data unencrypted.
claims.quote: Illuminate stored student data unencrypted.

claims.text: The FTC alleged that the threat actor first gained access to Illuminate's network on December 27, 2021.
claims.quote: the threat actor first gained access to Illuminate's network on December 27, 2021

claims.text: The FTC alleged that the threat actor remained undetected until January 8, 2022, when Illuminate discovered and terminated the access.
claims.quote: remained undetected in Illuminate's network until January 8, 2022, when Illuminate discovered and terminated their access

claims.text: The intrusion lasted 13 days.
claims.quote: With unfettered access to Illuminate's IO account for 13 days

claims.text: Illuminate was first warned of security vulnerabilities in its network 23 months before the intrusion began.
The FTC alleged that a third-party vendor's January 2020 assessment identified several major security vulnerabilities and assigned Illuminate an overall 'C' security grade.
claims.quote: The vendor's January 2020 assessment of Illuminate's network identified several major security vulnerabilities, provided recommended remediations, and assigned Illuminate an overall "C" security grade

claims.text: The FTC alleged that as of the vendor's February 2021 assessment, more than a year later, Illuminate still had not addressed its weak IAM practices and use of weak credentials.
claims.quote: more than a year after the January 2020 assessment, Illuminate still had not addressed its weak IAM practices and use of weak credentials

claims.text: The FTC alleged that the credentials used to breach the IO account belonged to a former employee with IAM administrator privileges who departed Illuminate in April 2018, over three and a half years before the breach.
claims.quote: belonged to a former employee, an IAM user with administrator privileges, who had departed Illuminate in April 2018, over three and a half years before the breach

claims.text: The FTC alleged that Illuminate did not disable or rotate the departed employee's key pair upon their departure or at any time in the three and a half years before the breach.
claims.quote: Illuminate did not disable nor rotate this former employee's key pair upon their departure, or anytime during the three and a half years prior to the breach

claims.text: The FTC alleged the breach compromised the personal information of more than 10.1 million students.
claims.quote: compromising the personal information of more than 10.1 million students

claims.text: Illuminate's breach was self-inflicted.
Illuminate's security posture was negligent and its failures were egregious.
The FTC alleged that Illuminate's internal breach notification procedure required notification to affected parties no later than 72 hours from the determination of a breach.
claims.quote: required notification to affected parties no later than 72 hours from the determination of a breach

claims.text: The FTC alleged that in contracts with New York and Connecticut school districts, Illuminate promised breach notification as early as 24 or 48 hours respectively from knowledge or discovery of a potential breach.
claims.quote: Illuminate promised to provide notifications of any breach or unauthorized release of student data as early as 24 or 48 hours, respectively, from the knowledge or discovery of a potential breach

claims.text: The FTC alleged that in October 2023, nearly two years after the breach, approximately 387,000 current and former students were newly identified as affected.
claims.quote: in October 2023, nearly 387,000 current and former students were newly identified as being affected by Illuminate's December 2021 - January 2022 data breach

claims.text: The gap between Illuminate's confirmation of the breach and its latest notifications was approximately 21 months, against an internal policy of 72 hours.
The FTC alleged that Illuminate and its third-party vendor confirmed the data breach on January 8, 2022.
claims.quote: Illuminate and its third-party vendor confirmed the data breach on January 8, 2022

claims.text: Illuminate paid a ransom to the threat actor.
claims.quote: Illuminate eventually agreed to pay an undisclosed amount in exchange for the receipt of the stolen data

claims.text: Illuminate never encrypted student personal information at any point in the relevant period.
claims.quote: stored, until at least January 2022, students' personal information on Illuminate's network in S3 buckets in plaintext, rather than encrypting the information

claims.text: In the Consent Agreement, Illuminate neither admitted nor denied the allegations in the draft Complaint.
claims.quote: statements by Respondent that it neither admits nor denies any of the allegations in the draft Complaint

claims.text: The final order requires multi-factor authentication for all employees and contractors accessing assets storing Covered Information, and that authentication may not use telephone or SMS-based methods and must be resistant to phishing attacks.
claims.quote: may not include telephone or SMS-based authentication methods and must be resistant to phishing attacks

claims.text: The final order requires Illuminate to terminate an authorized user's access within 30 days following a change in their need to know, including because of termination of employment.
claims.quote: terminate access within 30 days following a change in Authorized Users' need to know (including because of the termination of employment or contract)

claims.text: The final order requires encryption of databases in Covered Products both in transit over external networks and at rest.
claims.quote: Protecting by encryption, at a minimum, databases in Covered Products designed to store all Covered Information held or transmitted by Respondent both in transit over external networks and at rest

claims.text: The final order requires vulnerability scanning of networks containing Covered Information once every four months and annual penetration testing.
claims.quote: vulnerability scanning of Respondent's network(s) containing Covered Information once every four months

claims.text: The order was issued June 5, 2026 and terminates 10 years from the date of its issuance.
claims.quote: This Order will terminate 10 years from the date of its issuance

claims.text: This is the 31st FTC data-security enforcement action alleging failure to deprovision former-employee credentials since 2013.
claims.query: SELECT count(*) FROM ftc_enforcement WHERE allegation_class='credential_deprovisioning' AND year >= 2013

claims.text: The FTC alleged that Illuminate failed to employ effective vulnerability monitoring and patch management practices.
claims.quote: failed to employ effective vulnerability monitoring and patch management practices

claims.text: The FTC alleged that Illuminate's own post-breach analyses acknowledged that the compromised credentials were approximately 10 years old.
claims.quote: the employee's credentials were approximately 10 years old

claims.text: The FTC alleged that Illuminate could have prevented or mitigated the failures through readily available and relatively low-cost measures.
claims.quote: Illuminate could have prevented or mitigated these information security failures through readily available, and relatively low-cost, measures

claims.text: The FTC alleged that Illuminate's January 2020 vendor assessment specifically identified weak IAM practices, outdated software, use of weak credentials, and insecure system configurations.
claims.quote: The January 2020 assessment identified major security vulnerabilities including Illuminate's weak IAM practices, outdated software, use of weak credentials, and insecure system configurations.

claims.text: The FTC alleged the threat actor exfiltrated 787 SQL server backups.
claims.quote: exfiltrating 787 SQL server backups

claims.text: Each violation of the final order may result in a civil penalty of up to $51,744.
claims.quote: Each violation of such an order may result in a civil penalty of up to $51,744
